governing_requirement: Publish a closed, byte-verified MaskFactory release identity that proves the actual clean repository/build, one exact runtime provenance branch, complete contract and artifact catalogs, trusted role/revocation/journal state, and reproducible install/rollback identities without changing the frozen v1 wire contract.
prerequisites: MF-P6-09.07 remains open; publication evidence must bind the adopted executable crosswalk rather than invent a parallel compatibility claim.
existing_enforcement: The frozen release snapshot is strict, canonical-hashed and Ed25519-signed with a producer-release role and production trust-anchor check.

The validator requires dirty=false, the exact 12-schema set, exact schema name/id/path relationships, all three completion profiles, a self-hashed artifact-security policy, a current journal checkpoint, and the six required artifact kinds.

Bundle validation resolves contained regular files, verifies raw hashes and declared sizes, rejects path escape, symlink/reparse/hardlink-like paths, archive hazards, case aliases and unmanifested files.
Release/adoption validation already separates fixture-only evidence from published runtime evidence and rejects fixture publication as production authority.

confirmed_gaps.observed: maskfactory_release_snapshot has no runtime provenance field. It cannot select exactly one native_venv or container branch or bind interpreter/environment/CUDA/driver versus image/config/digest identities.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Replace repository_id, git_commit and git_tree with arbitrary well-formed values, retain dirty=false, then re-sign with the trusted producer-release key.
confirmed_gaps.interpretation: The signed declaration is internally authentic but no publisher compares it to the actual canonical repository, HEAD, tree or porcelain state.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Replace installer, install-manifest, verification-workflow and rollback/evidence IDs and hashes with invented well-formed values, then re-sign with the trusted producer-release key.
confirmed_gaps.interpretation: Installation and rollback fields have no relative paths, are not included in the release-root catalog, and are not independently resolved to actual bytes or execution evidence.

confirmed_gaps.observed: Key-set, rotation-policy, revocation-policy and journal validator identities are declared, but the release bundle does not enumerate and verify the underlying policy/registry/checkpoint evidence bytes as a closed catalog.

The repository contains conformance fixture builders and validators but no production publisher that observes the live Git/runtime/build state, materializes the exact release root, emits the signed publication decision, and refuses publication on drift.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement additive fail-closed production release publication evidence over the frozen v1 snapshot, binding actual Git, runtime, artifact, trust, journal, install and rollback observations without modifying the frozen v1 contracts.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. Candidate paths are read-only; src/maskfactory/validation.py is already dirty in the preserved 08.05 scope and must not be a worker candidate or mutation target. Allowed paths are disjoint from all held 09.01-09.07 work units.
acceptance_criteria: Publish a strict self-hashed and trusted-signed publication-evidence record binding the exact frozen release ID/payload/document hash and adopted 09.07 crosswalk ID/hash.
Observe and compare the canonical repository identity, exact 40-hex HEAD and tree, and an actually clean worktree including untracked files; a caller-supplied dirty=false cannot override observation.

Require exactly one typed runtime branch: native_venv binds executable/build, Python/platform, environment-lock, installed-distribution, CUDA/driver/GPU identities; container binds immutable image/config/runtime digests and rejects native-only fields.

Resolve every catalog row to contained regular bytes and close the catalog over wheel, node pack, OpenAPI, all schemas, semantic/crosswalk/completion profiles, package/ontology/workflow/fixture/evidence records, trusted role-key registry, revocation state and journal checkpoint evidence.
Regenerate OpenAPI and inventories deterministically and reject mismatch with packaged bytes, schema IDs/versions/hashes, entry points or node inventory.
Resolve installer, install manifest, verification workflow, rollback command and rollback verification evidence to exact cataloged bytes; use structured argv/IDs, never an untrusted shell string.
Reject self-signed, wrong-role, expired, not-yet-valid, substituted or revoked release keys and drift in key-set, rotation, revocation or checkpoint inputs.
Emit a closed publication decision with deterministic ordered reasons; any unknown, missing, duplicate, extra, mismatched, stale or fixture-only input denies publication.

Negative tests cover fake Git identity, dirty/untracked worktree, both/neither runtime branches, fake container provenance, changed environment lock, unmanifested or path-escaping bytes, stale/revoked trust, fake installer/rollback and non-reproducible canonical output.
Frozen v1 schemas, fixtures, validation.py and all held worker paths remain byte-identical. Full clean-build reconstruction remains MF-P6-10.04-owned and is not falsely claimed by this unit.

observed_verification.command: trusted re-sign probe: substitute producer repository_id/git_commit/git_tree
trusted re-sign probe: substitute installer/install-manifest/verification-workflow and rollback/evidence identities
python -m pytest -q tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py -k 'release_requires_exact_profile_documents or release_bundle_is_exact_byte_closed or archive_indirection'

claim_boundary: This audit proves existing frozen release validation strengths and isolates missing production-publication authority. It does not publish a release, complete MF-P6-10.01, prove a clean build/runtime, issue a runtime certificate, or claim Main adoption or end-to-end execution.
